status: wired (2026-07-23): AgentSpec logic lives in the shared core public/js/agent-spec-core.js (server façade src/agent-spec.js; sdk/pair-cli.mjs re-exports it); the Agent Studio mode and share-link minting resolve agents from this file via the committed source snapshot. An agent is DEFINED by its chat-input-pane controls, its intro + loading animations, its colour theme, its seed example questions, and the default quota a minted share-link token carries. Derive a new agent by copying one of these and changing those fields.

control_types.depth-slider: research depth → number of pipeline search rounds
control_types.toggle: a boolean request flag (web search, incognito, …) — id IS the flag
control_types.mode-select: choose the chat mode (Normal / Introspection / Agent Studio)

agents.name: Research
agents.tagline: The full signed-in deep-research assistant — DeepResearch.Se/rver.
agents.description: The server tier: one edge worker orchestrates the deterministic triage → search → gap → synthesis → validation pipeline, with identity, quotas, cloud storage and the full model catalog.
examples: What are the trade-offs between HNSW and IVF for vector search at 10M vectors?
Summarise the current evidence on GLP-1 drugs and cardiovascular outcomes.
Compare Cloudflare Workers, Deno Deploy and Bun for a low-latency edge API.

quota.note: Signed-in default window; a share link mints its own metered token.

agents.name: Secure
agents.tagline: The never-cloud tier — the research assistant that runs wholly in your browser. DeepResearch.Se/cure.
agents.description: The client tier: the server is in no data path. Provider calls go browser-direct on your own keys (or your local model server), the pipeline runs in the page, and all state rests sealed in browser-local storage. Its privacy is structural.

controls.placeholder: Ask — nothing leaves this device except the model call…

examples: Draft a privacy policy for a browser-only app that never sees my data.
Explain how AES-GCM sealing of local chat history works.
What can a static host observe about my session? What can't it?

quota.note: No server quota by default — Se/cure runs on your own keys. A share link may add a bounded, disclosed web-search grant.

agents.name: Under Construction
agents.tagline: A placeholder tier — the template you copy to start a brand-new agent.
agents.description: The minimal viable agent: a composer, a send button, and an honest 'not built yet' notice. It exists to be copied — change the name, add controls, pick a theme, and you have a new agent.

quota.note: A cautious default for a work-in-progress share link.

agents.name: Agent Studio
agents.tagline: The mode that builds agents — distil this site into a new flavour and publish it live.
agents.description: Formerly 'SDK mode'. Describe a flavour to distil from the Se/cure tier; the model plans against the DistillSDK manifest, reads the deployed source, builds a self-contained agent, and publishes it at /app/<slug>/. This is the pair-studio module made real.
examples: Build a single-purpose legal-research agent themed in deep blue.
Distil a stripped-down single-file Se/cure client with only a model picker.
Make a Swedish-language study assistant with a depth slider and no web search.

quota.note: Building is expensive; a share link to a builder gets a tighter default.
